title: Governance Pipeline Integrity — Commit Linkage, Automated Issue Lifecycle, and Verification Integration
summary: Close the verifiability gap between on-chain governance hashes and off-chain execution by linking commit SHAs to attestations, automating issue lifecycle from CEO evaluation output, and integrating verify.py with the tracking layer. Validated through 5-lens expert analysis (constitutional, systems, engineering, DAO comparison, security) with no disqualifying findings.
body: The governance pipeline currently stores keccak256 hashes on-chain with URIs pointing to the git repository. But there is no programmatic link between a specific git commit and its on-chain record. An observer can verify file content against a hash, but cannot determine which repository state produced that hash. Similarly, the Algorithmic Manager produces structured reasoning trees with milestones and conditions, but these remain static JSON blobs — they do not automatically generate trackable work items or close when conditions are verified. This proposal bridges three gaps: (1) commit-to-chain provenance, (2) evaluation-to-tracking automation, and (3) verification-to-closure integration. A 5-lens expert analysis validated the approach: constitutional law (CONDITIONAL PASS), systems thinking (BENEFICIAL), software engineering (NEEDS DESIGN — staged implementation), DAO governance comparison (AHEAD/NOVEL — no existing DAO has comparable pipeline), security (ACCEPTABLE RISK with hardening). All conditions from the expert analyses are incorporated into this proposal's design.

conditions: Auto-created issues must be in PENDING REVIEW state — Human Executor activates
verify.py recommends closure, Human Executor confirms — no auto-close
Divergence-issue workflow defined before first auto-created issue
On-chain record must not depend on GitHub for primary verification
Stage auto-close behind mandatory human review step
Monitor for Goodhart's Law effects (issue closure as proxy for governance quality)
verify.py needs test suite before auto-close deployment
Build issue_manager.py as standalone module with idempotency
Use URI-encoded commit SHA — no contract upgrade
Structured milestone manifests required in future proposals
--create-issues as opt-in flag, not default behavior
Shell injection prevention: use --body-file not interpolation

daoComparison.assessment: No existing DAO (Compound, MakerDAO, Nouns, Optimism) has: (1) AI constitutional evaluation with published reasoning trees, (2) commit-to-chain cryptographic linkage for governance, (3) programmatic semantic verification of execution. The full pipeline is unprecedented.

conditions: Branch protection rules on main (no force-push)
Issue bodies use summaries with links, not full reasoning trees
Separate self-attestation from deterministic verification in verify.py
Cryptographic issue binding via on-chain decision ID in issue body

proposedChanges.change: Add commitSha to reasoning tree metadata via git rev-parse HEAD. Encode in URI field as raw.githubusercontent.com/{repo}/{commitSha}/{path}. Add --create-issues flag that invokes issue_manager.py after evaluation.

Standalone module handling idempotent issue creation and verification-result posting. Dedup via canonical key in issue title [P{id}-{domain}-{index}]. Issues created with 'pending-review' label — Human Executor must remove label and activate. On APPROVE: creates milestone issues from structured milestones array. On MODIFY: creates condition issues for each condition. Posts evaluation summary (not full tree) as issue comment with link to full reasoning tree in repo.

Add --check-issues flag. On verification pass, posts verification results as issue comment with recommendation to close. Does NOT auto-close. Human Executor reviews and closes. Distinguish between deterministic checks (hash_match, file_exists, corpus_integrity) and self-attestation checks (modifyConditions string matching) in output.

Add post-evaluation step: if --create-issues flag passed and evaluation produces APPROVE/MODIFY, invoke issue_manager.py. Add permissions block for issues:write. Add push trigger on governance/decisions/ path for verification runs. Restrict governance pipeline to workflow_dispatch only (remove fork PR trigger for security).
Enable branch protection on main: no force-push, require status checks. Add Fine-Grained PAT with project scope as repository secret for Projects v2 board linkage.

Define and document: On divergence, all am-generated issues for that decision ID are closed with 'diverged' label and comment linking to on-chain divergence record. On partial acceptance, Human Executor relabels accepted issues as 'he-accepted' and closes rejected ones as 'he-rejected-diverged'. Divergence record references specific milestone IDs.

Proposals with multi-phase roadmaps must include a machine-parseable 'milestones' array in the proposal JSON, not just natural language in the body. Each milestone: {id, domain, description, phase, dependencies, targetDate, verificationType}. This enables reliable auto-creation without fragile natural language parsing.

phase1.name: Foundation
deliverables: Commit SHA in evaluate.py output and URI field
issue_manager.py with idempotent creation, pending-review workflow

phase2.name: Integration
deliverables: verify.py test suite (prerequisite for any future auto-close consideration)
Retroactive commit SHA linkage for existing decisions

deferredDecisions.decision: Full auto-close of issues by verify.py
deferredDecisions.trigger: verify.py has comprehensive test suite with adversarial cases AND 30+ days of human-review-then-close operation demonstrates zero false positives
deferredDecisions.rationale: Systems analysis identified Goodhart's Law risk and automation complacency. Constitutional analysis requires HE confirmation at every state transition. Staging auto-close behind proven correctness is the conservative path.

deferredDecisions.trigger: GitHub platform dependency becomes a demonstrated risk OR multi-member governance requires censorship-resistant backup
deferredDecisions.rationale: Security analysis recommends but current single-platform risk is LOW given on-chain hashes as trust root.

deferredDecisions.decision: Smart contract upgrade to add commitSha field to Attestation struct
deferredDecisions.trigger: Need for on-chain queryable commit SHA (e.g., verification contract that cross-checks GitHub state)
deferredDecisions.rationale: Engineering analysis confirms URI encoding achieves same goal without contract upgrade overhead.

estimatedCost: $0 direct cost. Implementation time: ~1 week Phase 1, ~2 weeks Phase 2.
externalDependencies: GitHub API availability; gh CLI in governance-engine workflow; Fine-Grained PAT for Projects v2 scope
